home.title: BibexPy V2
home.subtitle: Bibliometric data preparation
home.heroTitle: Harmonizing the Bibliometric Symphony of Scopus and Web of Science
home.heroSubtitle: BibexPy V2 turns raw database exports into clean, deduplicated and enrichment-ready datasets for VOSviewer, Biblioshiny and the wider bibliometrics workflow.
heroChecks: 100% self-hosted|Runs on your machine — no cloud upload
Audit-friendly outputs|Every step logged for methods sections
home.tagline: Scopus + Web of Science · DOI-based deduplication · Multi-stage matching
features: Conversion|CSV ↔ XLSX ↔ WoS TXT, BibTeX, RIS, VOSviewer.
Merging|DOI-based deduplication, Simple and Enhanced merge.
Rich filter|Year, journal, author, category, full-text, quality / missing data.
API + ML Enrichment|CrossRef, OpenAlex, Scopus, Unpaywall + TF-IDF predictions.
AI Disambiguation*|DeepSeek LLM for author name + affiliation normalization.
Export|WoS, VOSviewer, BibTeX, RIS, CSV, XLSX — filtered subset supported.
workflow: Project|Create a local workspace that keeps raw files, analyses, exports and audit history together.
Prepare|Upload Scopus CSV, Web of Science TXT or spreadsheets; BibexPy consolidates source tables automatically.
Merge Analyses|Run Simple, Enhanced or Smart matching as independent analyses and keep older runs available.
Records & Quality|Review the active dataset, filter records, inspect missing fields and manage snapshots.
Harmonize|Use API, ML and guided AI review to standardize metadata, authors and affiliations.
Export|Create tool-ready WoS, VOSviewer, Biblioshiny, BibTeX, RIS, CSV or Excel outputs.
Report|Keep an operation report and audit trail attached to the project.
home.workflowHeadline: From local project workspace to traceable bibliometric outputs.
home.workflowBody: The current workflow centers on one self-hosted project: prepare files, create repeatable merge analyses, review the active dataset, harmonize metadata, export to analysis tools, and keep an audit report.
home.ctaBody: Create a new project, upload Scopus CSV and Web of Science TXT files, and let BibexPy do the heavy lifting — deduplication, field merging, enrichment.
home.ctaSecondary: Just need a quick conversion? Try the standalone {tools}.
home.toolsBody: The Tools page is a lightweight utility area for one-off format conversion. Use it when you do not need a full project, merge history or analysis workspace.
home.capabilityBody: The homepage now leads with the work BibexPy actually removes from the researcher: file cleanup, source matching and reliable exports.
capabilities: Prepare|Clean source exports, validate columns and keep every raw file traceable.
Reconcile|Merge records with DOI logic, field harmonization and lost-record tracking.
Export|Deliver clean datasets to VOSviewer, Biblioshiny, reference managers and spreadsheets.
assurances: Reproducible|Every operation leaves an audit trail that can be reported in a methods section.
Local and private|Raw datasets stay on the machine unless you explicitly choose an external API.
Self-hosted|FastAPI and Next.js run as your own local research workspace.
Extensible|Add enrichment services, review rules and export targets as projects mature.
home.aboutBody: BibexPy is a self-hosted, open-source tool that automates the most tedious part of any bibliometric study: data preparation. It harmonizes Scopus and Web of Science records — two databases that describe the same publications with different field codes, formats and naming conventions — and exports clean datasets ready for VOSviewer, bibliometrix, CiteSpace and any reference manager.
home.aboutPoint1Body: Every bibliometric paper spends ~40% of its time on data cleanup. BibexPy handles the deduplication, field merging, lost-record tracking and audit-trail generation, so you can focus on analysis.
home.aboutPoint2Body: Every merge, filter, enrichment and disambiguation step is captured in an audit log. Snapshots are taken before destructive operations. Methodologically transparent — referee-friendly.
home.aboutPoint3Body: Your raw data never leaves your machine. Backend (FastAPI) + Frontend (Next.js) run locally. Optional API/LLM calls are opt-in and only sent for specific records.
home.citationBody: If you use BibexPy in academic work, please cite our SoftwareX paper:
home.citationApa: Kara, B. C., Şahin, A., & Dirsehan, T. (2025). BibexPy: Harmonizing the bibliometric symphony of Scopus and Web of Science. SoftwareX, 30, 102098.
home.authorsBody: BibexPy is developed and maintained by:

projects.title: Projects

data.title: Data
data.subtitle: Step 1 — Upload raw Scopus CSV and/or Web of Science TXT files. The system consolidates each source into a single Excel file.
data.readyDescription: Your raw data is uploaded and each source has been consolidated into a single Excel. Next step: merge two sources into one dataset in the Analyses page.
data.prepareDescription: The system consolidates each source (Scopus / WoS) into a single Excel file. This step does not require manual management. (Merging Scopus + WoS is done in the next step.)
data.addNewHint: Existing raw files are preserved — only consolidated source Excels are reset.
data.resetTitle: Reset preparation and add new data?
data.resetSubtitle: So you can add new files and re-run preparation
data.rawPreservedNote: raw files in your list — kept.
data.previousReset: Previous preparation reset (your raw files are kept)
data.previousResetHint: Upload new files, then click "Prepare" — old and new raw files together will produce new {scopusFile} and {wosFile} (one consolidated Excel per source).
data.stale: Preparation out of date — raw files changed. Click "Re-prepare" to update consolidated Excels.
data.preparedHint: "Prepare" combines all your raw files into a single {scopusFile} and {wosFile}.

analyses.title: Analyses
analyses.subtitle: Upload raw Scopus/WoS files and merge them into a single dataset with Smart Merge (DOI-based dedup) — preparation is automatic. Each merge is stored as an independent analysis.
analyses.newAnalysisDrawerHint: Choose an algorithm — the active analysis is preserved, a new folder is created.
analyses.listHint: Each merge is an independent analysis folder. The active analysis is used in Records / Filter / Quality pages.
analyses.otherAnalysesHint: Past analyses are preserved. Click "Activate" to use one in Records / Filter / Quality.
analyses.noAnalysisHint: Start your first analysis by choosing one of the algorithms below.

infoBanner.title: How does merging work?
infoBanner.body: Scopus and Web of Science describe the same publication with different fields/formats. This step matches the two sources by {doi}, keeps common records once, and resolves conflicts.
infoBanner.footer: Each merge is stored as an independent analysis — you can switch between them, view older ones, and delete if needed. No records are deleted — unmatched WoS/Scopus rows are moved to "Lost Records" files.

algoCard.rerunNote: The active analysis is preserved — a new analysis folder is created.
algoCard.escClose: Press ESC or click outside to close

simple.title: Simple Merge
simple.summary: Deletes Scopus row for DOI-matched records, keeps WoS row.
howItWorks: All WoS + Scopus records are concatenated into a single dataframe.
Records with DOIs are grouped; for duplicate DOIs only the first row (WoS) is kept.
For records without DOI: cleaned title + year exact match deduplication.
Fields come from a single source (WoS if available, otherwise Scopus) — no field merging.
useCases: When a fast exploratory analysis is needed
When data quality is already high in both sources
When manual cleanup will be done afterwards
limitations: No title fuzzy match — only exact alphanumeric
No year tolerance (online-first vs published difference)

enhanced.title: Enhanced Merge
enhanced.summary: Deduplication + fills empty fields from the other source (WC↔SC cross-fill).
howItWorks: All steps of Simple Merge are applied.
In DOI groups, fields are merged with 'merge_values()' — union of non-empty values (joined with ;).
WC ↔ SC cross-fill: if one is empty, fill from the other.
Author names normalized to WoS format.
useCases: When AB / DE missing in WoS; WC missing in Scopus
limitations: Which field came from which source is not tracked
Title+Year exact match carries false-positive risk (e.g., 'Introduction' titled editorials)

smart.title: Smart Merge
smart.summary: 6-stage multi-level matching + field preference rules + audit trail.
howItWorks: Phase 1 (Normalize): DOI, title, year, author surname, ISSN/PMID/UT reduced to canonical form.
Phase 2 (Blocking): Records grouped by year + first author surname letter (O(n²) → O(n·k)).

Phase 3 (Multi-stage matching): Stage 0 negative rules (PMID/ISSN mismatch → reject) → Stage 1 DOI exact (conf 1.0) → Stage 2 PMID exact (0.99) → Stage 3 Title JW ≥ 0.92 + Year±1 + Surname (0.95) → Stage 4 Journal+Volume+Pages (0.90) → Stage 5 Borderline JW ∈ [0.80, 0.92) (manual review).
Phase 4 (Field merge): Caputo 2024 fixed defaults — TC/CR/NR→WoS, AB/AU/AF/C1→Scopus, DE/ID→union, WC/SC→cross-fill.
Phase 5 (Audit): match_audit.xlsx + conflict_log.xlsx + borderline_queue.xlsx are produced.
Phase 6 (Borderline review): Manual approval UI or optional LLM decision.
useCases: Studies requiring academic reproducibility (SCI/SSCI manuscript writing)
Old publications without DOI (pre-2000) / inconsistent metadata
Systematic review data preparation (PRISMA-ScR compliant audit)
Studies open to audit trail / reviewer scrutiny

outputs.name: match_audit.xlsx
outputs.desc: Each match's DOI, stage, confidence, rationale

limitations: Strict 0.92 threshold for Title+Year+Surname may miss some duplicates (shown in borderline queue)

citation.title: Caputo, A., Pizzi, S., Pellegrini, M. M., & Dabić, M. (2024)
citation.journal: Automatic Merging of Scopus and Web of Science Data — Annals of Data Science, 11(3), 1023–1047.
related: Hammerton et al. (2013) — Rule-based deduplication. Database, bat086.
Borissov et al. (2022) — Deduklick. Systematic Reviews, 11, 172.
Winkler (1990) — Jaro-Winkler similarity. ASA Survey Research Methods.

merge.summaryNote: This analysis result is used in the Records & Filtering, Harmonization and Export steps.

narrative.title: Methods paragraph (copy-ready)
narrative.hint: Auto-generated summary you can paste into a manuscript's data preparation section.
narrative.template: On {date}, a database merging and deduplication process was conducted. A total of {scopus} records from Scopus and {wos} records from Web of Science were included in the procedure ({total_input} records in total). The Smart Merge algorithm identified {duplicates} duplicate records across the datasets. Following deduplication, the final consolidated dataset consisted of {total} unique records.

merge.staleHint: New raw files were added — the active analysis is based on older data. Run Smart Merge to create a new analysis.

classic.simpleDescription: DOI-based deduplication applied. For rows with the same DOI in WoS + Scopus, only the WoS row was kept; no field merging was done.
classic.enhancedDescription: Deduplication + cross-database field completion applied. Records representing the same publication across WoS and Scopus had their fields merged; empty fields were filled from the other source.
classic.simpleHint: Simple Merge does not merge fields — use Enhanced or Smart Merge for richer results.
classic.enhancedHint: Enhanced Merge does not record which field came from which source. For academic audit trail (match rationale, conflict decision), use Smart Merge.

simpleFieldRules.rule: DOI matching — first WoS row is kept among rows with the same DOI
If no DOI: cleaned title + year exact comparison
Come from a single source (WoS priority, otherwise Scopus) — no field merging

enhancedFieldRules.rule: Identifier matching — DOI / WoS UT / PubMed ID exact

enhancedFieldRules.field: AB, AU, AF, DE, ID, WC, SC
enhancedFieldRules.rule: merge_values() — union of non-empty values (; joined + dedup)
Cross-fill — if one is empty, completed from the other
WoS author format normalization (Smith J → 'Smith, J.')

smart.borderlineNote: {pending} uncertain pairs kept separate (total {total}). You can review them from the "Uncertain Pairs" prompt in this Smart Merge step.
smart.detailedReportNote: All match decisions, the stage and confidence each was made at, conflict resolutions and borderline pairs are written to the audit log. Keep for academic reproducibility.

files.title: Generated Files
files.lostNote: {wos} WoS · {scopus} Scopus rows did not match by DOI
files.smartAuditNote: Match details, conflict decisions and borderline queue

branch.title: What would you like to do now?
branch.exportBody: Download the merged data, export it and generate the methodology report — no filtering needed.
branch.continueBody: Filter records, check field quality, review uncertain pairs and enrich.

done.title: Merge complete 🎉
done.body: Your data is consolidated into a single dataset. Now export the results or generate a report. Open Advanced below if you want detailed editing.
done.exportBody: Download as WoS · VOSviewer · BibTeX · RIS · Excel.
done.reportBody: Generate the bibliometric methodology and summary report.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.hint: (optional) detailed editing, match details, generated files
advanced.filterBody: Filter records, fix fields, review uncertain pairs.
advanced.harmonizeBody: Unify and standardize author / institution / country names.

next.downloadBody: Export the results (WoS · VOSviewer · BibTeX · RIS · Excel) and report.
next.advancedBody: Filter, fix fields, harmonize — unlocks the advanced steps (3-4).

help.jw: Jaro-Winkler: a 0–1 string-similarity score for titles. Pairs at or above the threshold are treated as the same publication; lower pairs are kept separate. 0.88 is a balanced default.
help.blockingThreshold: Blocking threshold (0–1): how similar two names/affiliations must be to land in the same candidate group for review. Lower = wider net (more candidates, slower); higher = stricter.
help.autoApprove: Auto-approve threshold (0–1): pairs at or above this similarity are merged automatically without AI/manual review. Keep it high (e.g. 0.95) to avoid false merges.

borderline.title: Uncertain Pairs — Review (optional)
borderline.description: Smart Merge could not match these pairs with certainty (JW 0.80–0.92) and kept them SEPARATE automatically. You can merge the ones that are the same publication here.
borderline.gatePrompt: Smart Merge could not decide with certainty whether {pending} pairs are the same publication and kept them SEPARATE automatically. Do you want to manually review these uncertain pairs before completing the merge?
borderline.gateDismissedToast: Merge complete — uncertain pairs kept separate.
borderline.bulkConfirm: A snapshot will be taken and the dataset updated for accepted pairs. Continue?
borderline.noBorderline: ✓ No borderline pairs — Smart Merge resolved all matches with high confidence.
borderline.noFilterMatch: No pairs match the current filter.
borderline.noBorderlineHint: Smart Merge has not produced any borderline matches, or they have all been resolved.

records.title: Records & Filter
records.subtitle: Apply filters, manage records, check field quality.
records.deleteWarning: A snapshot is taken before delete — you can restore later.
records.nextStepSubtitle: Step 4 (Harmonization) is optional. If you don't need semantic clean-up, jump straight to Export.
records.nextStepAIBody: Merge author / affiliation / keyword variants that look the same but are written differently. Uses LLM where deterministic rules can't decide.
records.nextStepExportBody: Your dataset is already merged, filtered and quality-checked. Convert directly to WoS / VOSviewer / BibTeX / Excel / CSV.
records.tableHint: Tip: Click header → sort · Drag header edge → column width · Click row → detail

filter.authorsSubtitle: Substring search in AU — can add multiple
filter.fulltextPlaceholder: (machine OR deep) AND learning NOT survey

presets.title: Presets
presets.hint: Apply a filter first, then save.

quality.title: Data Quality Dashboard
quality.subtitle: Field-by-field fill rate
quality.fillFieldHint: Use API enrichment to fill empty values in this field
quality.healthScoreHint: Bibliometrically weighted: critical fields (authors, year, source, citations, references) count most; complementary fields least. Differs from the fill bar below, which weights all fields equally.
quality.healthHint: Average fill rate of DI, AB, TI, AU, PY fields
quality.barLegend: Left = total filled (all fields pooled); right = each field's missing share, by color

overview.title: General Overview
overview.hint: Dataset summary table (Biblioshiny-style) — paste into a report or download.

quality.fillHint: "Fill" → empty records are queried via their DOI against CrossRef · OpenAlex · Europe PMC · DataCite APIs. A snapshot is taken automatically and audit log is written.
quality.scanDesc: {n} field(s) have missing data — fill them automatically from CrossRef, OpenAlex, Europe PMC and DataCite.
quality.scanAllGood: All tracked fields look complete — nothing to fill.
quality.reportSummary: {enriched} of {scanned} records with a DOI were updated.
quality.reportDois: +{n} missing DOI(s) resolved from title lookup.
quality.startAllHint: Run API enrichment in parallel for every field with missing values
quality.startAllConfirm: Enrichment will be started for {n} fields. The jobs run in parallel and may take a little while.
quality.startAllNothing: Nothing to fill — all fields are complete
quality.startAllFreeNote: The enrichment sources used (Crossref, OpenAlex, Unpaywall) are free and consume no paid quota.

records.deleteRowConfirm: Delete this record? A snapshot is taken so it can be restored.

enrich.title: Harmonization
enrich.subtitle: Use LLM to standardize data where semantic decisions are needed — merge variants, generate missing fields.
enrich.infoBanner1: Records & Filter page fills fields from deterministic APIs (CrossRef, OpenAlex...).
enrich.infoBanner2: Harmonization solves places where a semantic decision is needed: "are these two authors the same person?", "are these two keywords the same concept?"
howSteps: Dataset is scanned, all unique values in the relevant field are extracted.
Candidate groups are formed with deterministic similarity (Jaro-Winkler / token-level).
Uncertain groups are sent to LLM — semantic decision is requested.
Suggestions are listed with confidence score; user approves one by one.
Approved ones are applied to dataset after snapshot; audit log is written.
enrich.devStatusBody: The infrastructure of the Author and Affiliation Disambiguation module can be used as-is for these tools (blocking + similarity + LLM + approval). We await your feedback for prioritization.

disambiguation.label: Variant Merging
disambiguation.description: Merging different spellings of the same thing into one standard

extraction.label: Information Extraction
extraction.description: Generating missing fields from existing text

authors.title: Author Disambiguation
authors.description: Merge different spellings of the same author (Smith J / Smith John A. / J. A. Smith) into one name. Decided via coauthor + affiliation + year hints.

affiliations.title: Affiliation Standardization
affiliations.description: Pull MIT / Massachusetts Institute of Technology / Mass Inst Tech variants to one standard institution. Campus/city differences are preserved.

keywords.title: Keyword Merging
keywords.description: Pull 'machine learning' / 'Machine Learning' / 'ML' variants in DE/ID fields to one normalized form. Makes word-count analysis healthy.

journals.title: Journal Name Standardization
journals.description: Merge abbreviation/full name differences like 'IEEE Trans Knowl Data Eng' and 'IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering'.

categories.title: Subject Category Prediction
categories.description: For records missing WC/SC field, predict WoS Category from Abstract. LLM fallback for cases not returned by APIs.

kw-from-ab.title: Abstract → Keywords
kw-from-ab.description: For records with empty DE but filled AB, generate 5-10 keywords from Abstract. Aligned with standard bibliometric vocabulary.

organizations.title: Organizations (parent)
organizations.description: Roll department/sub-unit addresses up to the parent university/institution so the collaboration network is built at the organization level.

countries.title: Countries
countries.description: Standardize country-name variants (USA / United States / US -> United States).

enrich.apiDescription: Fill missing fields from CrossRef / OpenAlex / Semantic Scholar.
enrich.mlDescription: Use machine learning models to enhance records.
enrich.fieldDescription: Fill a specific field's empty values via API sources.

disambiguate.title: Author Disambiguation
disambiguate.subtitle: Cluster duplicate authors and affiliations.
disambiguate.modeAutoDescription: Sends only suspicious authors to LLM — fast, cheap
disambiguate.modeBroadDescription: Asks LLM about all same-surname+initial groups
disambiguate.modeFullLLMDescription: Sends ALL author groups to LLM — expensive, comprehensive
disambiguate.fullLLMWarning: This mode sends ALL author groups to LLM — costs may increase 10x+ on large datasets.
disambiguate.howItWorksList: Deterministic blocking (same surname + first initial) + Jaro-Winkler similarity.|Only uncertain blocks go to DeepSeek (typically 5-10% of authors).|LLM returns cluster suggestions + confidence score + rationale.|Nothing is applied automatically — data stays unchanged until you approve. A snapshot is taken before apply.|Responses are cached; the same block won't be sent to LLM twice.
disambiguate.limitHint: Optional — scan only the first N records. Useful for cost-controlled testing. Leave empty to scan all.
disambiguate.applyConfirm: {n} clusters will be applied to the dataset. A snapshot will be taken. Continue?
disambiguate.experimentalWarning: EXPERIMENTAL — Merge author name + affiliation variants via DeepSeek LLM. All suggestions await manual approval; applied to data only after a snapshot is taken.
disambiguate.llmNotConfigured: LLM API key is not configured.
disambiguate.settingsHintAfter: page — add the provider and API key.
disambiguate.noProposalsHint: No proposals yet. Click the "Scan" button above.
disambiguate.splitHeader: Splits — same name, likely different people
disambiguate.splitDesc: This name splits into field-disjoint groups; each becomes a separate author (suffix a/b/c). Largest group kept plain.
disambiguate.mergeDesc: Variants of the same {subject}, unified to one canonical form.
disambiguate.scanHint: Scan finds two things: same person written differently (merge) and same name that are different people (split). Everything needs your approval.
disambiguate.scanHintStd: Scan finds variants of the same {subject} and unifies them to one canonical form. Everything needs your approval.

export.title: Export
export.subtitle: Convert the active dataset into formats expected by bibliometric tools (WoS / VOSviewer / BibTeX / RIS / CSV / XLSX).
export.browserNotSupported: This browser doesn't support folder picker. Switch to path entry mode.
export.subsetExported: Subset of this filter will be exported
export.noPresetsHint: No presets yet. You can save a preset in the Filter page.
export.opensBrowserPicker: Pick the destination folder in your browser
export.browserSafetyNote: Files are written directly by your browser — the backend never sees the path.
export.pathNote: Provide an absolute path where files will be saved. Created automatically if missing. The backend writes directly to disk since it runs on this machine.

formatInfo.wos: Web of Science plain-text format — the de-facto standard for science-mapping workflows
formatInfo.vos: Tab-separated map+network file ready for VOSviewer co-authorship / co-citation visualizations
formatInfo.bib: BibTeX entries with full metadata — works in any LaTeX / reference-manager toolchain
formatInfo.ris: RIS reference format — universal import for desktop reference managers
formatInfo.xlsx: Spreadsheet with all fields and types preserved (numbers, dates, multi-value cells)
formatInfo.tsv: Tab-separated dump — safer than CSV when fields contain commas or quotes

duplicate.title: {format} already exists
duplicate.body: {count} previous {format} export{plural} found. What would you like to do?
duplicate.replaceHint: Old files are removed, only the new one stays.
duplicate.keep: Keep both — add a new timestamped file
duplicate.keepHint: Old files are preserved. The new file gets a new name.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage API keys, LLM settings and other configurations.
settings.modelNotInList: This model is not in this provider's preset list — manual value.
settings.maskedKeyNote: Current key is masked. Don't touch if you don't want to change.

STORAGE_DIR.label: Project Storage Folder
STORAGE_DIR.hint: All projects are stored here. If you change it, move existing projects to the new folder manually. A backend restart is required.

SCOPUS_API_KEY.label: Scopus API Key
SCOPUS_API_KEY.hint: Needed only for Scopus enrichment (Elsevier, institutional/paid). All other sources are free.

SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY.label: Semantic Scholar API Key
SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY.hint: Optional. Works without a key; a key only raises the rate limit (free).

UNPAYWALL_EMAIL.label: Unpaywall Email
UNPAYWALL_EMAIL.hint: Optional. Used for open-access PDF links; Unpaywall only asks for an email (free).

CROSSREF_EMAIL.label: CrossRef Email (recommended)
CROSSREF_EMAIL.hint: Optional but recommended. Gives higher throughput via CrossRef's polite pool (free).

LLM_PROVIDER.label: LLM Provider
LLM_PROVIDER.hint: Used by the disambiguation module. Any OpenAI-compatible API works.

LLM_BASE_URL.label: LLM Base URL
LLM_BASE_URL.hint: If provider is 'Custom', enter the full endpoint URL here (e.g. https://api.together.ai/v1).

infoNotes: Settings are written to the .env file.
A backend restart may be required for some changes to take effect.
API keys are shown masked; to keep a value unchanged, leave its field untouched.

pathStatus.notDir: Exists but is not a directory

report.title: Operation Report
report.subtitle: Summary of all operations performed in this project

rawLogs.title: Raw Change Logs
rawLogs.desc: The complete, untouched operation log — every merge, filter, edit and enrichment, in order. Download as Markdown, plain text or PDF.

methodology.title: LLM Manuscript Report
methodology.desc: Turn your operation log into an English data-preparation methodology paragraph, ready to paste into the Methods section of your paper. It is based only on the steps you actually performed.
methodology.generating: Sending the log to the model and writing the methodology…
methodology.notConfigured: No language model is configured. Add an API key in
methodology.noOperations: Perform at least one step to generate a report.
methodology.draftNote: AI-generated — review the numbers against your data before publishing. The BibexPy citation is included in the text.
methodology.failed: Could not generate the methodology report.

audit.title: Operation history
audit.subtitle: Merge, delete, enrichment etc. operations are recorded automatically.
audit.clearConfirm: Delete ALL operation records? This cannot be undone.
audit.restoreConfirm: The dataset will be reverted to this snapshot. Any changes made after it will be lost.

titles.prepareReset: Preparation reset — {n} processed files deleted (raw files kept)

recordDetail.title: Record detail
recordDetail.editHint: Edit any field below — empty fields can be filled in manually too. Saved to the active dataset (a snapshot is taken).

bulk.enrichDescription: All missing fields of selected records will be queried from CrossRef · OpenAlex · Europe PMC · DataCite.

errors.analysis_delete_failed: Analysis could not be deleted (file may be locked, try again later)
errors.folder_access_denied: You don't have access to this folder
errors.csv_encoding_failed: Could not read CSV encoding (tried UTF-8 / cp1254 / latin-1)
errors.disambiguation_disabled_hint: Disambiguation is off (enable it in Settings)
errors.file_empty: File is empty — no records to convert
errors.not_a_folder: Exists but is not a folder
errors.target_folder_not_absolute: Target folder must be an absolute path (e.g. C:\... or /home/...)
errors.scopus_convert_failed: Scopus CSV → XLSX conversion failed. Is the file really a Scopus CSV?
errors.no_records_to_delete: No records specified to delete (uids, dois or indices)
errors.wos_convert_failed: WoS TXT → XLSX conversion failed. Is the file really WoS plain text?
errors.output_file_missing: Output produced but file not found
errors.job_not_found_or_uncancelable: Job not found or cannot be canceled
errors.no_files_to_process: No CSV or TXT files to process (XLSX is used directly)
errors.format_files_exist: files from this format already exist
errors.no_source_data: No source data found — go to Step 1 (Preparing) and upload Scopus CSV or Web of Science TXT.
errors.not_prepared: Data is not prepared yet. Go to Step 1 (Preparing) and run “Prepare” to build the datasets before merging.
errors.no_merged_data: No merged dataset — run Merge first
errors.llm_key_missing: LLM API key not set — add it in Settings
errors.no_operations: No operations to report yet. Perform at least one step first.
